builtin_function.title: Construct Vector
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with listed elements.

builtin_function.title: Generate Vector
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector generated from a function with a variable (default x) running from min to max. The 4th argument is either the step between each value of the variable, if the 6th argument is 1 or if the value is 1 (default), negative, or not an integer and the 6th argument is -1 (default), or the number of elements.

builtin_function.title: Colon Function (number sequence vector)
builtin_function.description: Returns a sequence of numbers as a vector.

builtin_function.title: Sort
builtin_function.description: Returns a sorted vector.

builtin_function.title: Rank
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with values of elements replaced with their mutual ranks.

builtin_function.title: Flip
builtin_function.description: Reverses the order of elements in a matrix or vector. If dimension is 1, the order of rows is reversed, if 2 column order is changed, and if 0 (default) both are changed.

builtin_function.title: Vector Part
builtin_function.description: Returns a part of a vector between two positions. Negative indexes are counted from the back of the vector. If the second index is beyond the end of the vector, zeroes are appended to the returned vector.

builtin_function.title: Dimension
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of elements in a vector.

builtin_function.title: Combine Vectors
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with the elements from multiple vectors.

builtin_function.title: Construct Matrix
builtin_function.description: Returns a matrix with specified dimensions and listed elements. Omitted elements are set to zero.

builtin_function.title: Convert Matrix to Vector
builtin_function.description: Puts each element of a matrix in vertical order in a vector.

builtin_function.title: Matrix Part
builtin_function.description: Returns a part of a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Rows
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of rows in a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Columns
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of columns in a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Extract Row as Vector
builtin_function.description: Returns a row in a matrix as a vector.

builtin_function.title: Extract Column as Vector
builtin_function.description: Returns a column in a matrix as a vector.

builtin_function.title: Elements
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of elements in a matrix or vector.

builtin_function.title: Element
builtin_function.description: Returns the element at specified position in a matrix (row and column) or vector (index).

builtin_function.title: Transpose
builtin_function.description: Returns the transpose of a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Identity
builtin_function.description: Returns the identity matrix of a matrix or with specified number of rows/columns.

builtin_function.title: Determinant
builtin_function.description: Calculates the determinant of a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Permanent
builtin_function.description: Calculates the permanent of a matrix. The permanent differs from a determinant in that all signs in the expansion by minors are taken as positive.

builtin_function.title: Adjugate (Adjoint)
builtin_function.description: Calculates the adjugate or adjoint of a matrix.

builtin_function.title: Cofactor
builtin_function.description: Calculates the cofactor of the element at specified position.

builtin_function.title: Matrix Inverse
builtin_function.description: Calculates the inverse of a matrix. The inverse is the matrix that multiplied by the original matrix equals the identity matrix (AB = BA = I).

builtin_function.title: Load CSV File
builtin_function.description: Returns a matrix imported from a CSV data file.

builtin_function.title: Export To CSV File
builtin_function.description: Exports a matrix to a CSV data file.

builtin_function.title: Magnitude
builtin_function.description: Calculates the magnitude of a value. This function returns the same value as abs() for all values except vectors.

builtin_function.title: Entrywise Function
builtin_function.description: Calculates a new matrix or vector using each separate element in matrix/vector 1 and the corresponding (in the same row and column) elements in matrix/vector 2. An unlimited number of matrices/vectors can be specified, with each matrix/vector argument followed by the corresponding variable used in the function argument.

builtin_function.title: Norm (length)
builtin_function.description: Calculates the norm/length of a vector.

builtin_function.title: Dot Product
builtin_function.description: Calculates the dot product of two vectors.

builtin_function.title: Set Intersection
builtin_function.description: Returns a sorted vector with the elements that exists in both vectors.

builtin_function.title: Set Difference
builtin_function.description: Returns a sorted vector with the elements that exists only in the first vector.

builtin_function.title: Unique
builtin_function.description: Returns a sorted vector with no repititions.

builtin_function.title: Count Occurrences
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of times the specified value appears in a vector.

builtin_function.title: Set Union
builtin_function.description: Combines two vectors and returns a sorted vector with no repititions (unless multiset is true).

builtin_function.title: Is Member
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with values of the first vector replaced with 1 or 0 (true or false), indicating if the corresponding value is found in the second vector.

builtin_function.title: Is Subset
builtin_function.description: Returns true if the second vector contains all values of the first vector. If strict is true, equal vectors are not allowed.

function.title: Cross Product
function.description: Calculates the cross product of two 3-dimensional vectors.

function.title: Scalar Triple Product
function.description: Calculates the scalar-valued triple product of three 3-dimensional vectors.

function.title: Symmetric Set Difference
function.description: Returns a sorted vector with the elements that exists in either the first or the second vector, but not in both.

builtin_function.title: Factorial
builtin_function.description: Calculates the factorial of an integer. Multiplies the argument with every lesser positive integer (n(n-1)(n-2)...2*1). Can also be entered as a number followed by one exclamation mark.

builtin_function.title: Double Factorial
builtin_function.description: Calculates the double factorial of an integer. Multiplies the argument with every second lesser positive integer (n(n-2)(n-4)...). Can also be entered as a number followed by two exclamation marks.

builtin_function.title: Multifactorial
builtin_function.description: Calculates the multifactorial of an integer. Multiplies the argument with every x lesser positive integer (n(n-x)(n-2x)...). Can also be entered as a number followed by three or more exclamation marks.

function.title: Hyperfactorial
function.description: Calculates the hyperfactorial of an integer. Multiplies the argument raised by itself with every lesser positive integer raised by themselves (1^1 * 2^2 ... n^n).

function.title: Superfactorial
function.description: Calculates the superfactorial of an integer. Multiplies the factorial of the argument with the factorial of every lesser positive integer (1! * 2! ... n!).

function.title: Permutations (Variations)
function.description: Returns the number of possible arrangements of an ordered list with a number of objects to choose from and a list size. If there are three objects (1, 2 and 3) that are put in a list with two positions, the alternatives are [1, 2], [2, 1], [1, 3], [3, 1], [2, 3] and [3, 2], and thus the number of permutations is 6.

function.title: Combinations
function.description: Returns the number of possible arrangements of an unordered list with a number of objects to choose from and a list size. If there are three objects (1, 2 and 3) that are put in a list with place for two, the alternatives are [1, 2], [1, 3], and [2, 3], and thus the number of combinations is 3.

function.title: Derangements
function.description: Returns the number of possible rearrangements of an ordered list, of a certain size, where none of the objects are in their original positions. If the original list is [1, 2, 3], the possible derangements are [2, 3, 1] and [3, 1, 2], and thus the number of derangements is 2.

builtin_function.title: Factors
builtin_function.description:
Performs integer or polynomial factorization, and returns the factors in a vector.

Mode determines the return value for integer factorization as follows.
0: all prime factors
1: factors without duplicates
2: factors and exponents in a matrix with two columns
3: the exponent after each factor in vector

builtin_function.title: Bernoulli Number/Polynomial
builtin_function.description: Returns the nth Bernoulli number or polynomial (if the second argument is non-zero).

builtin_function.title: Euler's Totient Function
builtin_function.description: Counts the positive integers up to a given integer n that are relatively prime to n.

builtin_function.title: Modular Exponentiation
builtin_function.description: Finds the modular inverse for negative exponents, and is otherwise equivalent to mod(a^b, c). For negative exponents the greatest common divisor of the numerator and the denominator must be 1.

builtin_function.title: Prime Counting Function
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of prime numbers less than or equal to the specified number.

builtin_function.title: Next Prime Number
builtin_function.description: Returns the next prime number greater than or equal to the specified number.

builtin_function.title: Previous Prime Number
description: Returns the largest prime number smaller than or equal to the specified number.

builtin_function.title: Prime Numbers Less Than or Equal
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector containing all the prime numbers less than or equal to the specified number.

builtin_function.title: Round
builtin_function.description:
Round to nearest integer or decimal. If the second argument is zero, the value is rounded towards the nearest integer, otherwise the value is rounded to the corresponding number of digits to the right (if positive) or left (if negative) of the decimal point.

Available rounding methods (3rd argument): half away from zero (0), half to even (1), half to odd (2), half toward zero (3), half up (4), half down (5), half random (6), toward zero (7), away from zero (8), up (9), down (10)

builtin_function.title: Get Digit
builtin_function.description: Returns digit at specified position (index of first digit left of decimal sign is zero).

builtin_function.title: Set Digit
builtin_function.description: Sets the digit at specified position (index of first digit left of decimal sign is zero).

function.title: Clip
function.description: Clips or limits the input value to be between the lower and upper bounds.

builtin_function.title: Number Base
builtin_function.description:
Returns a value from an expression using the specified number base (radix). For bases between -62 and 62 full mathematical expressions (including operators and functions) are supported, while for other bases the specified expression is converted to a single number.

Bases ≤ 36 use digits 0-9 and A-Z (case insensitive).

Bases between 37 and 62 uses case sensitive letters (0-9, A-Z, a-z) as digits ('z' equals 61).

Bases over 62 use Unicode characters as digits, with the character code as value (e.g. '0' equals 48). Escaped characters are in this case supported (e.g. '\0' = 0, '\523' = 523, '\x7f' = 127).

Negative bases use the same digits as the corresponding positive bases and the digits used for non-integer bases are determined by rounding the base away from zero. Bases that are not real numbers by default use digits 0-9 and A-Z.

The set of digits used can be selected using the third argument (defaults to 0 for automatic selection). Set it to 1 for digits 0-9 and A-Z, 2 for 0-9, A-Z and a-z, 3 for Unicode digits, and 4 for phonewords (e.g. ABC=2, DEF=3, etc.), or enter a text string with all digits placed in ascending order (e.g. "0123456789") and optionally separated by semicolon (to enable multiple equivalent digits, e.g. "0;aA1;bB2;cC3"). When the set of digits is manually selected, the specified expression is always converted to a single number.

builtin_function.title: Binary
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from a binary expression. If two's complement is true, numbers beginning with '1' are interpreted as negative binary numbers using two's complement.

builtin_function.title: Octal
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from an octal expression.

builtin_function.title: Decimal
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from a decimal expression.

builtin_function.title: Hexadecimal
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from a hexadecimal expression. If two's complement is true, numbers beginning with 8 or higher are interpreted as negative hexadecimal numbers using two's complement.

builtin_function.title: Bijective base-26
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from an expression in bijective base-26. Conversion in the opposite direction is also supported.

function.title: Fibonacci Number
function.description: Returns the n-th term of the Fibonacci sequence.

function.title: Multiples
function.description: Returns all multiples of a value in the specified range.

builtin_function.title: Bessel Function of the First Kind
Bessel Function of the Second Kind
Riemann Zeta
builtin_function.description: Calculates Hurwitz zeta function if the second argument is not 1.

function.title: Kronecker Delta
function.description: Returns 0 if i != j and 1 if i = j.

builtin_function.title: Heaviside Step Function
builtin_function.description: Discontinuous function also known as "unit step function". Returns 0 if x < 0, 1 if x > 0, and 1/2 if x = 0.

builtin_function.title: Dirac Delta Function
builtin_function.description: Returns 0 if x is non-zero, and infinity if x is zero.

builtin_function.title: Square Root
builtin_function.description: Returns the principal square root (for positive values the positive root is returned).

builtin_function.title: Cube Root
builtin_function.description: Returns the third real root.

builtin_function.title: Nth root
builtin_function.description: Returns the real root. For negative values the degree must be odd. Complex values are not allowed.

builtin_function.title: Lambert W Function (Omega Function, Product Log)
builtin_function.description: Returns the inverse function for x*e^x as ln() does for e^x. Only the principal branch and real valued results are currently supported.

function.title: Square root (x * pi)
function.description: Returns the non-negative square root of x * pi

builtin_function.title: Four-quadrant Inverse Tangent
builtin_function.description: Computes the principal value of the argument function applied to the complex number x+iy.

builtin_function.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point
builtin_function.description: Reads a number in a IEEE 754 floating-point format. The number will be read as a binary number, unless it contains digits other than 1 or 0. If the third argument (exponent bits) is set to zero, the standard number of exponent bits will be used (e.g. 8 for 32-bit format).

builtin_function.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point Bits
builtin_function.description: Converts a value to a number in a IEEE 754 floating-point format and returns the number corresponding to the binary representation. If the third argument (exponent bits) is set to zero, the standard number of exponent bits will be used (e.g. 8 for 32-bit format).

builtin_function.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point Error
builtin_function.description: Calculates the error (the difference between the original and the converted value) when converting a value to a IEEE 754 floating-point format. If the third argument (exponent bits) is set to zero, the standard number of exponent bits will be used (e.g. 8 for 32-bit format).

builtin_function.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point Components
builtin_function.description: Converts a value to a number in a IEEE 754 floating-point format and returns sign, exponent, and significand in a vector. If the third argument (exponent bits) is set to zero, the standard number of exponent bits will be used (e.g. 8 for 32-bit format).

builtin_function.title: IEEE 754 Floating-Point Value
builtin_function.description: Returns the closest value that can be represented by a IEEE 754 floating-point format. If the third argument (exponent bits) is set to zero, the standard number of exponent bits will be used (e.g. 8 for 32-bit format).

builtin_function.title: Roman Number
builtin_function.description: Returns the value of a roman number.

builtin_function.title: Distance Between GPS Coordinates
builtin_function.description: Calculates the distance between two geodetic coordinates using Vincenty's formulae (with datum WGS 84), or, in case of failure, the Haversine forumla. Each coordinate can be specified using a numerical value (representing decimal degrees), an angle (e.g. with degree unit), or a text string ending with N, S, E, or W (S for negative latitude, W for negative longitude).

function.title: Convert to/from Q Format (Fixed Point)
function.description: Converts to or from fixed point number. Corresponds to Qm.n format where m is the second argument and n is the third argument. m does not include the sign bit. If the value is an integer, and the fourth argument is false, the value is converted from fixed point, otherwise to.

function.title: Body Mass Index (BMI)
function.description:
Calculates the Body Mass Index. The resulting BMI-value is sometimes interpreted as follows (although varies with age, sex, etc.):

Underweight < 18.5
Normal weight 18.5-25
Overweight 25-30
Obesity > 30

Note that you must use units for weight (ex. 59kg) and length (ex. 174cm).

function.title: RAID Space
function.description: Calculates RAID array disk capacity usable for data storage. If the combination of number of disks and RAID level is invalid, zero is returned. Supported RAID levels are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 1+0/10, 0+1, 5+0/50, 6+0/60, and 1+6. Stripes are optional and only used for nested RAID levels (except 1+0).

function.title: Depth of Field
function.description: Returns the estimated distance between the nearest and the farthest objects that are in acceptably sharp focus in a photo. Enter focal length (e.g. 50 mm) and distance (e.g. 5 m) with units, and f-stop without unit (2.8, 4.0, 5.6, etc.). Specify either a cicle of confusion diameter limit (e.g. 0.05 mm) or the sensor size of the camera - 0="35mm", 1="APS-H", 2="APS-CN" (Nikon, Pentax, Sony), 3="APS-C" (Canon), 4="4/3" (Four Thirds System), or 5='1"' (Nikon 1, Sony RX10, Sony RX100) - for a diameter based on d/1500.

argument.title: Circle of confusion or sensor size

function.title: American Wire Gauge Cross-Section Area
function.description: For gauges larger than 0000 (4/0), please use negative values (00=-1, 000=-2, 0000=-3, 00000=-4, etc). For conversion to AWG, use an equation (e.g. awg(x) = 20 mm^2).

function.title: American Wire Gauge Diameter
function.description: For gauges larger than 0000 (4/0), please use negative values (00=-1, 000=-2, 0000=-3, 00000=-4, etc). For conversion to AWG, use an equation (e.g. awgd(x) = 5 mm).

function.title: Drill Bit Size
function.description: Returns drill bit gauge number or letter, if argument is fraction or diameter value with length unit, or drill bit diameter (with length unit), if argument is an integer or an upper-case character (quoted)

builtin_function.title: Min
builtin_function.description: Returns the lowest value.

builtin_function.title: Max
builtin_function.description: Returns the highest value.

builtin_function.title: !statistics!Mode
builtin_function.description: Returns the most frequently occurring value.

function.title: Range
function.description: Calculates the difference between the min and max value.

function.title: Interquartile Range
function.description: Calculates the difference between the first and third quartile.

function.title: Number of Samples
function.description: Returns the number of samples.

builtin_function.title: Random Number
builtin_function.description: Generates uniformly distributed pseudo-random numbers. Returns real numbers between 0 and 1, if ceil is zero (default), or integers between 1 and (including) ceil.

function.title: Random Number Between Limits
function.description: Returns uniformly distributed random integers between (including) bottom and top.

function.title: Simple Linear Fit
function.description: Returns the linear function, for a set of x and y values, estimated using simple linear regression with a single explanatory variable and the ordinary least squares method. If the vector of y values is empty, the first argument is used for sequential y values (with x values 1, 2, ..., n). It is possible to pass both x and y values in a two column matrix as the first argument.

function.title: Quadratic Fit
function.description: Fit data to a polynomial of degree 2, using least-squares method. If the vector of y values is empty, the first argument is used for sequential y values (with x values 1, 2, ..., n). It is possible to pass both x and y values in a two column matrix as the first argument.

function.title: Cubic Fit
function.description: Fit data to a polynomial of degree 3, using least-squares method. If the vector of y values is empty, the first argument is used for sequential y values (with x values 1, 2, ..., n). It is possible to pass both x and y values in a two column matrix as the first argument.

function.title: Binomial Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability mass or cumulative distribution function of the binomial distribution.

function.title: Exponential Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the exponential distribution.

function.title: Logistic Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the logistic distribution.

function.title: Normal Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the normal distribution.

function.title: Pareto Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Pareto distribution.

function.title: Poisson Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Poisson distribution.

function.title: Rayleigh Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Rayleigh distribution.

function.title: Rayleigh Tail Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density p(x) at x for a Rayleigh tail distribution with scale parameter sigma and a lower limit. (from Gnumeric)

function.title: Gamma Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the gamma distribution.

function.title: Chi-Square Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the chi-square distribution.

function.title: Weibull Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Weibull distribution.

function.title: Beta Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the beta distribution.

function.title: Student's t-distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Student's t distribution.

function.title: F-distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the F-distribution.

function.title: Cauchy Distribution
function.description: Returns the probability density or cumulative distribution function of the Cauchy distribution.

builtin_function.title: Construct Date
builtin_function.description: Returns a date. Available calendars are gregorian (1), hebrew (2), islamic (3), persian (4), indian (5), chinese (6), julian (7), milankovic (8), coptic (9), ethiopian (10), egyptian (11). The Chinese year uses an epoch of 2697 BCE and chinese leap months are indicated by adding 12 to the month number (e.g. leap month 4 = 16).

builtin_function.title: Days between two dates
builtin_function.description:
Returns the number of days between two dates.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360, 1: real days (default), 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

builtin_function.title: Years between two dates
builtin_function.description:
Returns the number of years (fractional) between two dates.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360, 1: real days (default), 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

builtin_function.title: Time Value
builtin_function.description: Returns the time part, in fractional hours, of a date and time value.

builtin_function.title: Unix Timestamp to Date
builtin_function.description: Returns the local date and time represented by the specified Unix timestamp (seconds, excluding leap seconds, since 1970-01-01). Supports time units.

builtin_function.title: Lunar Phase
builtin_function.description: Returns the lunar phase, as a number between 0 and 1, for the specified date. This value corresponds to an angle between 0 and 360 degrees. 0 represents new moon, 0.5 full moon, and 0.25 and 0.75 quarter moons.

builtin_function.title: Find Lunar Phase
builtin_function.description: Returns the date when the specified lunar phase occurs. The function searches forward beginning at the specified date. The lunar phase is specified as a number between 0 and 1, where 0 represents new moon, 0.5 full moon, and 0.25 and 0.75 quarter moons. Angle values are also allowed (e.g. π rad = 180° which corresponds to a value of 0.5). Values above 1, without unit, are interpreted as degrees.

function.title: Add Time
function.description: Adds a time value to a date. The value can be positive or negative, but must use a unit based on seconds (such as day and year).

builtin_function.title: Interval
builtin_function.description: Returns a closed interval with the specified endpoints. If exclude endpoints is true, the next closest floating point values toward the midpoint will be used (the exact values depends on the current internal precision), which can be useful for avoidance of invalid results at the endpoints.

builtin_function.title: Uncertainty
builtin_function.description: Specifies the absolute or relative (default) uncertainty/error of a value.

builtin_function.title: Lower Endpoint (interval)
builtin_function.description: Returns the lower endpoint of a numerical interval.

builtin_function.title: Upper Endpoint (interval)
builtin_function.description: Returns the upper endpoint of a numerical interval.

builtin_function.title: Midpoint (interval)
builtin_function.description: Returns the midpoint between the endpoints of a numerical interval, or the value part of a value with uncertainty/error.

builtin_function.title: Get Uncertainty
builtin_function.description: Returns the absolute (default) or relative uncertainty/error of a numerical value.

builtin_function.title: Unicode Value
builtin_function.description: Encodes a Unicode character or text string using the selected format. Supported encodings are UTF-8 (0), UTF-16 (1), and UTF-32 (2). If the third argument is true, each separate code unit (8, 16, or 32 bits depending on encoding) is placed in a vector.

builtin_function.title: Replace
builtin_function.description: Replaces a certain value in an expression with a new value. The expression is calculated before the replacement if the fourth argument is true.

builtin_function.title: Strip Units
builtin_function.description: Removes all units from an expression. No unit conversion or prefix changes are performed before the removal.

builtin_function.title: Select Vector Elements
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with the elements that fulfil the specified condition.

builtin_function.title: Find Vector Elements
builtin_function.description: Returns a vector with the positions of elements that fulfil the specified condition.

builtin_function.title: Save as Variable or Function
builtin_function.description:
Stores a value in a variable or saves an expression as a function.

A function is created if the name includes parentheses (e.g. "f()"). Optionally the function arguments can be specified in the name (e.g. "save(a+b,f(a,b))"). Otherwise the function arguments are expected to be referred to in the expression using \x, \y, \z, \a, \b, ..., or x, y, z (e.g. "save(x+y,f())").

If a function was created, the processed function expression is returned as a text string, otherwise the value is returned.

The ":=" operator (e.g. var1:=10) is a shortcut for this function.

builtin_function.title: RPN Stack Register
builtin_function.description: Returns the value of a RPN stack register.

builtin_function.title: RPN Stack Vector
builtin_function.description: Returns the RPN stack as a vector.

builtin_function.title: Is Number
builtin_function.description: Returns true if evaluated argument value is explicitly a real, complex, or infinite number (has number type).

builtin_function.title: Is Real
builtin_function.description: Returns true if evaluated argument value is explicitly a real number (has number type with zero imaginary part).

builtin_function.title: Is Rational
builtin_function.description: Returns true if evaluated argument value is explicitly a rational number (has rational type).

builtin_function.title: Is Integer
builtin_function.description: Returns true if evaluated argument value is explicitly an integer (has integer type).

builtin_function.title: Represents Number
builtin_function.description: Returns true if value is or represents a number (scalar without unit). False negatives are allowed.

builtin_function.title: Represents Real
builtin_function.description: Returns true if value is or represents a real number. False negatives are allowed.

builtin_function.title: Represents Rational
builtin_function.description: Returns true if value is or represents a rational number. False negatives are allowed.

builtin_function.title: Represents Integer
builtin_function.description: Returns true if value is or represents an integer. False negatives are allowed.

builtin_function.title: LaTeX
builtin_function.description: Returns a value from a LaTeX amsmath and/or siunitx expression. Note that any expression that begins and ends with $ and contains a backslash is interpreted using this function.

builtin_function.title: If...Then...Else
builtin_function.description: Tests a condition and returns a value depending on the result. Vectors can be used for argument 1 and 2, instead of nested functions.

argument.title: Expression if condition is NOT met

builtin_function.title: Bitwise Shift
builtin_function.description: Applies logical or arithmetic bitwise shift to an integer. The second argument specifies the number of steps that each binary bit is shifted to the left (use negative values for right shift).

builtin_function.title: Bitwise Complement (Not)
builtin_function.description: Applies bitwise NOT to an integer of specified bit width and signedness (use 1 for signed and 0 for unsigned). If bit width is zero, the smallest necessary number of bits (of 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, ...) will be used.

builtin_function.title: Bit Rotation
builtin_function.description: Applies circular bitwise shift to an integer of specified bit width and signedness (use 1 for signed and 0 for unsigned). The second argument specifies the number of steps that each binary bit is shifted to the left (use negative values for right shift). If bit width is zero, the smallest necessary number of bits (of 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, ...) will be used.

builtin_function.title: Set Bit
builtin_function.description: Set binary bit at specified position. The index of the least significant bit is 1. Specify bit width and signedness (use 1 for signed and 0 for unsigned) to allow sign changes when the most significant bit is set.

builtin_function.title: Set Multiple Bits
builtin_function.description: Set binary bits at specified range with binary bits from an integer (index 1 to length of range). The index of the least significant bit is 1. Specify bit width and signedness (use 1 for signed and 0 for unsigned) to allow sign changes when the most significant bit is set.

builtin_function.title: Get Bit
builtin_function.description: Returns the binary bit at the specified position. The index of the least significant bit is 1. If last index is non-zero the bits from (first) position to, and including, last position are returned as a new binary number.

builtin_function.title: Bit Population Count
builtin_function.description: Returns the number of 1 bits in the binary representation of the specified integer.

builtin_function.title: Summation
builtin_function.description: Corresponds to the summation symbol. Adds terms for each x ranging from the lower to the upper limit.

builtin_function.title: Product of a sequence
builtin_function.description: Corresponds to the product symbol. Multiplies factors for each x ranging from the lower to the upper limit.

builtin_function.title: Solve differential equation
builtin_function.description: Solves a differential equation and returns the value of y(x). The derivative in the equation should be in the format diff(y, x). Only first-order differential equations are currently supported.

function.title: Solve for two variables
function.description: Solves two equations with two unknown variables. Returns the value of the first variable.

function.title: Find Linear Function
function.description: Finds the linear function for the straight line between two distinct points.

builtin_function.title: Limit
builtin_function.description: Returns the two-sided limit of the function if direction is zero, limit from left (below) if direction is -1, or limit from right (above) if direction is +1.

function.title: Limit with Multiple Variables
function.description: Returns limit of a function for multiple variables. The limit is calculated recursively, for one variable in each iteration, with the result of each iteration used as function in the next.

builtin_function.title: Logarithmic Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of 1/ln(x).

builtin_function.title: Exponential Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of e^x/x.

builtin_function.title: Sine Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of sin(x)/x.

builtin_function.title: Cosine Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of cos(x)/x.

builtin_function.title: Hyperbolic Sine Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of sinh(x)/x.

builtin_function.title: Hyperbolic Cosine Integral
builtin_function.description: The integral of cosh(x)/x.

builtin_function.title: Fresnel Integral S
builtin_function.description: The integral of sin(pi*x^2/2).

builtin_function.title: Fresnel Integral C
builtin_function.description: The integral of cos(pi*x^2/2).

function.title: Circle Area
function.description: Calculates the area of a circle using the radius.

function.title: Circle Circumference
function.description: Calculates the circumference of a circle using the radius.

function.title: Volume of Rectangular Prism
function.description: Calculates the volume of a prism with rectangular base.

function.title: Surface Area of Rectangular Prism
function.description: Calculates the surface area of a prism with rectangular base.

function.title: Volume of Triangular Prism
function.description: Calculates the volume of a prism with triangular base.

function.title: Pyramid Volume
function.description: Calculates the volume of a 3-dimensional shape standing on a rectangular base and terminating in a point at the top.

function.title: Parallelogram Area
function.description: Calculates the area of a four-sided figure whose opposite sides are both parallel and equal in length.

function.title: Parallelogram Perimeter
function.description: Calculates the perimeter of a four-sided figure whose opposite sides are both parallel and equal in length.

function.title: Trapezoid Area
function.description: Calculates the area of a four-sided figure with two parallel sides.

function.title: Elasticity
function.description:
Calculates the demand elasticity. Also works for supply elasticity, income elasticity, cross-price elasticity, etc. Just replace demand with supply, or price with income...

e.g. elasticity(100-x^2, 3) calculates the demand elasticity when the price is 3 for the function "Q = 100 - x^2" where x is the default price variable.

function.title: Sum-of-Years Digits Depreciation
function.description: Calculates the sum-of-years digits depreciation for an asset based on its cost, salvage value, anticipated life, and a particular period. This method accelerates the rate of the depreciation, so that more depreciation expense occurs in earlier periods than in later ones. The depreciable cost is the actual cost minus the salvage value. The useful life is the number of periods (typically years) over which the asset is depreciated.

function.title: Straight Line Depreciation
function.description:
Determines the straight line depreciation of an asset for a single period.

Cost is the amount you paid for the asset. Salvage is the value of the asset at the end of the period. Life is the number of periods over which the asset is depreciated. SLN divides the cost evenly over the life of an asset.

function.title: Present Value
function.description:
Returns the present value of an investment.

If type = 1 then the payment is made at the beginning of the period. If type = 0 (or omitted) it is made at the end of each period.

function.title: Nominal Interest Rate
function.description: Calculates the nominal interest rate from a given effective interest rate compounded at given intervals.

function.title: Zero Coupon
function.description: Calculates the value of a zero-coupon (pure discount) bond.

function.title: Treasury Bill Yield
function.description: Returns the yield for a treasury bill.

function.title: Treasury Bill Price
function.description: Returns the price per $100 face value for a treasury bill.

function.title: Treasury Bill Equivalent
function.description: Returns the bond equivalent yield for a treasury bill.

function.title: Interest paid on a given period of an investment (ISPMT)
function.description: Calculates the interest paid on a given period of an investment.

function.title: Payment for a loan
function.description:
Returns the amount of payment (negative) each period for a loan based on a constant interest rate and constant payments (each payment is equal amount).

If type = 1 then the payment is made at the beginning of the period. If type = 0 (or omitted) it is made at the end of each period.

Note that the interest rate here refers to the rate for each period and if you calculate with an annual rate, each period will be interpreted as a whole year. To get monthly payments divide the annual interest rate by 12 and enter the total number of months (12 times number of years) in the periods field.

function.title: Rate of investment
function.description:
Calculates the rate of return.

If type = 1 then the payment is made at the beginning of the period. If type = 0 (or omitted) it is made at the end of each period.

Note that the optional guess is needed because there can be more than one valid result. It defaults to 10%.

function.title: Periods of an investment
function.description:
Calculates number of periods of an investment based on periodic constant payments and a constant interest rate.

Type defines the due date. 1 for payment at the beginning of a period and 0 (default) for payment at the end of a period.

function.title: Periods for investment to attain desired value
function.description: Returns the number of periods needed for an investment to attain a desired value.

function.title: Payment of an annuity going towards principal (PPMT)
function.description:
Calculates the amount of a payment of an annuity going towards principal.

Type defines the due date. 1 for payment at the beginning of a period and 0 (default) for payment at the end of a period.

function.title: Effective Interest Rate
function.description: Calculates the annual net interest rate for a nominal interest rate.

function.title: Future Value
function.description:
Computes the future value of an investment. This is based on periodic, constant payments and a constant interest rate.

If type = 1 then the payment is made at the beginning of the period. If type = 0 (or omitted) it is made at the end of each period.

function.title: Return on continuously compounded interest
function.description: Calculates the return on continuously compounded interest, given the principal, nominal rate and time in years.

function.title: Compound
function.description: Returns the value of an investment, given the principal, nominal interest rate, compounding frequency and time.

function.title: Payment of an annuity going towards interest (IPMT)
function.description:
Calculates the amount of a payment of an annuity going towards interest.

Type defines the due date. 1 for payment at the beginning of a period and 0 (default) for payment at the end of a period.

function.title: Interest rate for a fully invested security
function.description:
Returns the interest rate for a fully invested security.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Dollar Fraction
function.description: Converts a decimal dollar price into a dollar price expressed as a fraction.

function.title: Dollar Decimal
function.description: Converts a dollar price expressed as a fraction into a dollar price expressed as a decimal number.

function.title: Amount received at maturity
function.description:
Returns the amount received at the maturity date for an invested security.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360. The settlement date must be before maturity date.

function.title: Discount rate for a security
function.description:
Returns the discount rate for a security.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Accrued interest of security paying at maturity
function.description:
Returns the accrued interest for a security which pays interest at maturity date.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Accrued interest of security with periodic interest payments
function.description:
Returns accrued interest for a security which pays periodic interest.

Allowed frequencies are 1 - annual, 2 - semi-annual or 4 - quarterly. Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Number of coupons to be paid
function.description:
Returns the number of coupons to be paid between the settlement and the maturity.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Price per $100 face value of a discounted security
function.description:
Calculates and returns the price per $100 face value of a discounted security. The security does not pay interest at maturity.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Price per $100 face value of a security
function.description:
Calculates and returns the price per $100 face value of a security. The security pays interest at maturity.

Basis is the type of day counting you want to use: 0: US 30/360 (default), 1: real days, 2: real days/360, 3: real days/365 or 4: European 30/360.

function.title: Level-Coupon Bond
function.description: Calculates the value of a level-coupon bond.
